[2018-05-12 00:30] Deleted User (Deleted User): how helpful is it to think of zemora as alt econ
[2018-05-12 00:39] liadahlia (Lia): well, rather than an alt econ, isn't it more like a win condition

[2018-05-12 00:39] Deleted User (Deleted User): well econ is a win condition tbh
[2018-05-12 00:40] liadahlia (Lia): i think of econ as a way to get to a win condition
[2018-05-12 00:40] liadahlia (Lia): a typical "econ" win condition would be to have a shit ton of attackers
[2018-05-12 00:40] xyotsuba: Lol dude in that case everything is a win condition
[2018-05-12 00:40] Deleted User (Deleted User): like you can think of getting zemora in a similar way as getting econ
[2018-05-12 00:40] liadahlia (Lia): i suppose with zemora you can think of getting conduits as getting econs
[2018-05-12 00:41] xyotsuba: @Deleted User that definition drones are a win condition which is just wrong
[2018-05-12 00:41] Deleted User (Deleted User): you get it with good absorb, shit attackers, good soak, and you get it early on in the game
[2018-05-12 00:41] Deleted User (Deleted User): ok i wasn;t sure what the def of win con is exactly
[2018-05-12 00:41] Deleted User (Deleted User): you also get it to deny absorb
[2018-05-12 00:42] liadahlia (Lia): win con is kinda abstract, sorta like if you win, you won because of this unit more so than any other unit
[2018-05-12 00:42] liadahlia (Lia): that's a bit of a vague definition though
[2018-05-12 00:42] xyotsuba: The most basic win condition in prismata is having more dmg than the opponent can defend (in non-breachproof games)
[2018-05-12 00:44] xyotsuba: Certain units can qualify as win conditions because they are so impactful that if you successfully activate the unit condition u have a very high chance of winning. So the unit itself becomes the win condition.
[2018-05-12 00:45] Deleted User (Deleted User): i have made a ranked list of win conditions
[2018-05-12 00:45] wilson0862 (Wilson): I'm sure it'll be just as good as your other lists.
[2018-05-12 00:45] Deleted User (Deleted User): it is exactly as good as one other list
[2018-05-12 00:47] liadahlia (Lia): ampo is a win condition
[2018-05-12 00:47] liadahlia (Lia): lucina is a win condition
[2018-05-12 00:47] liadahlia (Lia): big units, generally
[2018-05-12 00:47] liadahlia (Lia): i suppose shadowfang is also a win con, to a lesser extent
[2018-05-12 00:48] xyotsuba: Its more accurate to say the win condition in amporilla games is to get a 4+ tarsier amporilla without getting breached
[2018-05-12 00:48] xyotsuba: But u can shorthand it to say just “ampo is my win con”
[2018-05-12 00:49] xyotsuba: For zemora u need to survive the buildtime and make enough conduits to fire every turn. But u can just say “zemora is my win con”
[2018-05-12 00:50] xyotsuba: However if you try to do an ampo or zemora strat without actually achieving their condition they will be useless
[2018-05-12 00:50] Deleted User (Deleted User): getting 10 doomed drones is my wincon tbh
[2018-05-12 00:50] liadahlia (Lia): yeah, getting 10 doomed drones insta win you the game
[2018-05-12 00:51] xyotsuba: Lol cant tell if sarcasm or not
[2018-05-12 00:52] f300xen (f0x): is tarsier a win condition
[2018-05-12 00:52] liadahlia (Lia): technically it can be
[2018-05-12 00:53] xyotsuba: Tarsier is just an attacker. It isnt impactful enough to be a win condition
[2018-05-12 00:53] masn6811 (Masne98888): is the time bank a win condition?
[2018-05-12 00:53] masn6811 (Masne98888): is your opponent's internet connection a win condition?
[2018-05-12 00:53] liadahlia (Lia): having 10 tarsiers in BSO is probably a win con
[2018-05-12 00:53] liadahlia (Lia): i think win con is relative
[2018-05-12 00:53] xyotsuba: Those are loss conditions i guess
[2018-05-12 00:54] xyotsuba: @Lia that just falls under having more attack than opponent can defend
[2018-05-12 00:54] masn6811 (Masne98888): nah, often having 7 tarsiers in BSO wins, but having 9 loses
[2018-05-12 00:54] liadahlia (Lia): well, that is the only win con in BSO
[2018-05-12 00:54] liadahlia (Lia): i don't really know BSO tbh, just an example
[2018-05-12 00:55] mtanzer: 7 is the best number of tarsiers in bso, can confirm

[2018-05-12 00:55] mtanzer: using "win condition" in prismata seems weird
[2018-05-12 00:55] mtanzer: I'm used to seeing it in hearthstone context since the decks are asymmetric
[2018-05-12 00:55] xyotsuba: Because u don’t build a deck
[2018-05-12 00:55] liadahlia (Lia): because your win con is also your opponent's wincon
[2018-05-12 00:55] mtanzer: so players legitimately have different win conditions

[2018-05-13 11:01] itsotter: wavered on turn 11 here, advice? vIdMt-9wDnB
[2018-05-13 11:01] itsotter: specifically re: holding lancetooth vs attacking with it
[2018-05-13 11:02] itsotter: this situation comes up a lot and i always just try to guess which is better but i don't have a great rule of thumb for it
[2018-05-13 11:02] itsotter: intuitively i always want to make the attack and buy prompt defense but i know it's wrong if i can buy sufficient attack by holding instead
[2018-05-13 11:03] itsotter: what "sufficient attack" is though, idk
[2018-05-13 11:05] amalloy: holding lancetooth is usually pretty bad. it's something you would only do if you need to do so to defend, or maybe if you very badly want to slow the game down because you're guaranteed to win the long game
[2018-05-13 11:06] itsotter: i know it's -1 value when you have a wall to absorb on, but the defense tempo letting me buy splitter + a rhino that wouldn't die on defense seemed good
[2018-05-13 11:06] amalloy: holding back 2 attack in order to buy more attack for next turn is less pressure, not more
[2018-05-13 11:06] amalloy: because you'll have to hold some of those attackers back anyway, if holding lancetooth was the only way to get them out
[2018-05-13 11:06] wilson0862 (Wilson): you almost always hold omega.
[2018-05-13 11:07] wilson0862 (Wilson): Why does your thought process not apply to omega, amalloy?
[2018-05-13 11:07] itsotter: i did ultimately make the swing just because i was like "i'm killing ossifieds, that will make his red hard to spend"
[2018-05-13 11:07] awaclus: Omega absorbs 5, Lance 3
[2018-05-13 11:08] f300xen (f0x): omega is 5/3 or 2 difference, lance is 3/2 or 1 difference
[2018-05-13 11:09] wilson0862 (Wilson): Oh, absorb vs damage.
[2018-05-13 11:09] awaclus: 5 absorb vs 3 damage
[2018-05-13 11:09] itsotter: however, if i hold lancetooth this turn to get 2 more attack onto the field, i don't think it can be assumed that it's useless attack because i'll have to hold it anyway since that's how i got it out
[2018-05-13 11:09] itsotter: next turn i could attack with everything and buy prompt defense to cover it
[2018-05-13 11:10] itsotter: creating a swing turn like that seems good to me, i think it's worth going -1 value in the short term
[2018-05-13 11:10] itsotter: all other things being equal
[2018-05-13 11:12] amalloy: i don't think you're often in that situation, where one turn the only way you can buy attackers is by holding lancetooth, and then on the next turn you somehow have enough defense to protect them while attacking with the lancetooth. if your opponent is attacking for normal numbers that just doesn't happen
[2018-05-13 11:12] amalloy: if the defense is good enough for that, then this turn you can defend the lancetooth and buy a smaller but nonzero amount of attack
[2018-05-13 11:13] wilson0862 (Wilson): If you're absorbing on wall, yes you probably want to swing with lancetooth. Usually I see the decision between absorbing on splitter or lancetooth though, where I would typically lean toward absorbing on lancetooth.
[2018-05-13 11:13] extratricky: yeah there's no real reason to hold tooth on turn 11 in that game
[2018-05-13 11:13] extratricky: you just have to buy WR to defend which is fine
[2018-05-13 11:14] itsotter: well, in my example, i could have bought splitter plus a rhino that doesn't die, and in the event that i swing, i can only buy wall plus a rhino that does die
[2018-05-13 11:14] itsotter: so i am adding zero attack instead of two
[2018-05-13 11:15] itsotter: adding two attack seems far better than adding zero attack on a given turn
[2018-05-13 11:15] extratricky: well the biggest problem is that you only really add two attack if you get to attack with everything next turn, but absorbing on tooth means you lost 3 defensive tempo from soaking with wall
[2018-05-13 11:15] itsotter: like i did the swing but this is not an obvious question to me like it seems to be to you guys
[2018-05-13 11:15] amalloy: attacking for two is far better than attacking for zero
[2018-05-13 11:16] amalloy: and how are you defending next turn anyway, if you buy the attackers? it's not easy for me to see
[2018-05-13 11:16] wilson0862 (Wilson): I guess I actually have to look at the replay to see why holding tooth then swinging with tooth is possible. 🤔
[2018-05-13 11:16] extratricky: like how often do you hold splitter to buy splitter and then get to attack with the original splitter on the next turn
[2018-05-13 11:16] extratricky: if you can then holding splitter can be better than buying wall, but usually you can't
[2018-05-13 11:17] amalloy: like you can lose both your ossified drones and also buy a rhino that dies
[2018-05-13 11:18] amalloy: but that really isn't great. you seem kinda dead to me after holding the tooth back
[2018-05-13 11:18] wilson0862 (Wilson): Why? They're way up in attack.
[2018-05-13 11:19] itsotter: i was gonna say i think i win handily both lines
[2018-05-13 11:19] itsotter: but i would still rather have a clear idea of the theory behind swinging and buying prompt defense vs holding a tooth to add more attack
[2018-05-13 11:19] extratricky: it's also worth noting that the 2 damage is killing either an ossy or a rhino which is a lot better than just 2 damage
[2018-05-13 11:19] amalloy: maybe i didn't play it out very carefully. you seem up in attack but down in absorb, econ, and defensive tempo
[2018-05-13 11:19] itsotter: does it depend on the current inflation levels?
[2018-05-13 11:20] itsotter: like adding permanent attack is better if the game hasn't hit very high inflation yet?
[2018-05-13 11:20] extratricky: it depends on how much the defensive tempo hurts you on the following turn I would say
[2018-05-13 11:20] extratricky: absorbing on tooth means you have to replace the wall with absorb, so you're starting the next turn with 0 defense instead of 3
[2018-05-13 11:20] extratricky: if you can defend like that then you can consider holding for attack
[2018-05-13 11:21] extratricky: it's hard for any tooth game to be low enough inflation for a pure inflationary view to tell you to hold
[2018-05-13 11:21] extratricky: since tooth denies absorb the inflation will tend toward the higher end
[2018-05-13 11:22] extratricky: but if they're very overdefended it could be a consideration
